256â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257â†’    ) {
258â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259â†’        if (
260â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
261â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
262â†’            // e.g.
263â†’            //
264â†’            // class A : B, C,
265â†’            //     D
266â†’            // or
267â†’            // class A : B, C({
268â†’            // }), D
269â†’            //
270â†’            // but not
271â†’            //
272â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
273â†’            // })
274â†’            !(
275â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
277â†’                )
278â†’        ) {
279â†’            // put space after :
280â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
281â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282â†’                if (
283â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
284â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
285â†’                ) {
286â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
287â†’                }
288â†’            }
289â†’            // put entries on separate lines
290â†’            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
291â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
292â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
293â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
294â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
295â†’                ) {
296â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
297â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
298â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
299â†’                        emit = emit,
300â†’                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
301â†’                    )
302â†’                }
303â†’            }
304â†’        }
305â†’    }
306â†’
307â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
308â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
309â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
310â†’
311â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312â†’        node: ASTNode,
313â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315â†’    ) {
316â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
317â†’            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320â†’                else -> false
321â†’            }
322â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
323â†’                // rearrange
324â†’                //
325â†’                // a, b, value(
326â†’                // ), c, d
327â†’                //
328â†’                // to
329â†’                //
330â†’                // a, b,
331â†’                // value(
332â†’                // ),
333â†’                // c, d
334â†’
335â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337â†’                if (
338â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340â†’                ) {
341â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342â†’                }
343â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
344â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347â†’                if (
348â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351â†’                    // value(
352â†’                    // ), // a comment
353â†’                    // c, d
354â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355â†’                ) {
356â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357â†’                }
358â†’            }
359â†’        }
360â†’    }
361â†’
362â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
363â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
364â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
365â†’
366â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
367â†’        node: ASTNode,
368â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
369â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370â†’    ) {
371â†’        node
372â†’            .treeParent
373â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
374â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
375â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
376â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
377â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
378â†’            ?.let {
379â†’                // rewriting
380â†’                // """
381â†’                //     text
382â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
383â†’                // to
384â†’                // """
385â†’                //     text
386â†’                // _
387â†’                // """.trimIndent()
388â†’                emit(
389â†’                    node.startOffset,
390â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
391â†’                    true,
392â†’                )
393â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
394â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
395â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
396â†’                }
397â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
398â†’            }
399â†’    }

401â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
404â†’        //     find matching rToken
405â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406â†’        // return false
407â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410â†’            // special cases:
411â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
412â†’            // })
413â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414â†’            // 2, 3)
415â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416â†’        }
417â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421â†’        }
422â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423â†’            return true
424â†’        }
425â†’        return false
426â†’    }

428â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
429â†’        node: ASTNode,
430â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
431â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
432â†’    ) {
433â†’        val p = node.treeParent
434â†’        if (
435â†’            // check
436â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
437â†’            // and
438â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
439â†’            // only
440â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
441â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
442â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
443â†’            // permit
444â†’            // when {
445â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
446â†’            //     })
447â†’            // }
448â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
449â†’            // permit
450â†’            // when (this) {
451â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
452â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
453â†’            //     -> true
454â†’            // }
455â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
456â†’        ) {
457â†’            return
458â†’        }
459â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
461â†’        }
462â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
463â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
464â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
465â†’        }
466â†’    }
467â†’
468â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
469â†’        node: ASTNode,
470â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
471â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
472â†’        indent: String,
473â†’    ) {
474â†’        emit(
475â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
476â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
477â†’            true,
478â†’        )
479â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
480â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
481â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
482â†’        }
483â†’    }
484â†’
485â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
486â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
487â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
489â†’        indent: String? = null,
490â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
491â†’    ) {
492â†’        emit(
493â†’            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
494â†’            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
495â†’            true,
496â†’        )
497â†’        LOGGER.trace {
498â†’            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
499â†’        }
500â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
501â†’            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
502â†’            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
503â†’        }
504â†’    }

506â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
507â†’        for (child in node.children()) {
508â†’            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
509â†’                val v = child.text
510â†’                if (v == "\n") {
511â†’                    return true
512â†’                }
513â†’            }
514â†’        }
515â†’        return false
516â†’    }

518â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
519â†’        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
520â†’        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
521â†’            textContains('\n')
522â†’        } else {
523â†’            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
524â†’                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
525â†’        }
526â†’    }

528â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
529â†’
530â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
531â†’
532â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
533â†’        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
534â†’            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
535â†’
536â†’    /**
537â†’     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
538â†’     *     for (foo in listOf(
539â†’     *         "foo-1",
540â†’     *         "foo-2"
541â†’     *     )) { ... }
542â†’     * but reject:
543â†’     *     for (
544â†’     *         foo in listOf(
545â†’     *             "foo-1",
546â†’     *             "foo-2"
547â†’     *         )
548â†’     *     ) { ... }
549â†’     */
550â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
551â†’        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
552â†’            return false
553â†’        }
554â†’        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
555â†’            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
556â†’        }
557â†’        require(elementType == LPAR) {
558â†’            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
559â†’        }
560â†’
561â†’        var node: ASTNode? = this
562â†’        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
563â†’            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
564â†’                return false
565â†’            }
566â†’            node = node.nextSibling { true }
567â†’        }
568â†’        return true
569â†’    }

571â†’    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
572â†’        node: ASTNode,
573â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
574â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
575â†’    ) {
576â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
577â†’            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578â†’            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
579â†’                return
580â†’            }
581â†’            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
582â†’            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
583â†’            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
584â†’            val wrapBlock =
585â†’                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
586â†’                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
587â†’                    )
588â†’            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
589â†’                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
590â†’                    endOfBlock,
591â†’                    autoCorrect,
592â†’                    emit,
593â†’                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
594â†’                )
595â†’            }
596â†’        }
597â†’    }
598â†’
599â†’    private companion object {
600â†’        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
601â†’        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
602â†’        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
603â†’            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
604â†’                RTOKEN_SET.types,
605â†’            ).toMap()
606â†’    }
607â†’}

 48â†’/**
 49â†’ * Linting trailing comma for declaration site.
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 52â†’ */
 53â†’public class TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule :
 54â†’    StandardRule(
 55â†’        id = "trailing-comma-on-declaration-site",
 56â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 57â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 58â†’                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 59â†’                mode = ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
 60â†’            ),
 61â†’            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62â†’        ),
 63â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY),
 64â†’    ) {
 65â†’    private var allowTrailingComma = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 66â†’
 67â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 68â†’        allowTrailingComma = editorConfig[TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_PROPERTY]
 69â†’    }
 70â†’
 71â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 72â†’        node: ASTNode,
 73â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 74â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 75â†’    ) {
 76â†’        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 77â†’        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 78â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 79â†’            CLASS -> visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 80â†’            DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 81â†’            FUNCTION_LITERAL -> visitFunctionLiteral(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 82â†’            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 83â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 84â†’            WHEN_ENTRY -> visitWhenEntry(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 85â†’            else -> Unit
 86â†’        }
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(
 90â†’        node: ASTNode,
 91â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        val inspectNode = node
 95â†’            .children()
 96â†’            .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
 97â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
 98â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
 99â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
100â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
101â†’            emit = emit,
102â†’        )
103â†’    }
104â†’
105â†’    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_DECLARATION_SITE && allowTrailingComma
106â†’
107â†’    private fun visitFunctionLiteral(
108â†’        node: ASTNode,
109â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111â†’    ) {
112â†’        val inspectNode = node
113â†’            .children()
114â†’            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ARROW }
115â†’            ?: // lambda w/o an arrow -> no arguments -> no commas
116â†’            return
117â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
118â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
119â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
120â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
121â†’            emit = emit,
122â†’        )
123â†’    }
124â†’
125â†’    private fun visitValueList(
126â†’        node: ASTNode,
127â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
128â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
129â†’    ) {
130â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
131â†’            node
132â†’                .children()
133â†’                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
134â†’                ?.let { inspectNode ->
135â†’                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
136â†’                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
137â†’                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
138â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
139â†’                        emit = emit,
140â†’                    )
141â†’                }
142â†’        }
143â†’    }

145â†’    private fun visitTypeList(
146â†’        node: ASTNode,
147â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149â†’    ) {
150â†’        val inspectNode = node
151â†’            .children()
152â†’            .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
153â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
154â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
155â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
156â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
157â†’            emit = emit,
158â†’        )
159â†’    }
160â†’
161â†’    private fun visitWhenEntry(
162â†’        node: ASTNode,
163â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165â†’    ) {
166â†’        val psi = node.psi
167â†’        require(psi is KtWhenEntry)
168â†’        if (psi.isElse || psi.parent.cast<KtWhenExpression>().leftParenthesis == null) {
169â†’            // no commas for "else" or when there are no opening parenthesis for the when-expression
170â†’            return
171â†’        }
172â†’
173â†’        val inspectNode = node
174â†’            .children()
175â†’            .first { it.elementType == ARROW }
176â†’        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
177â†’            inspectNode = inspectNode,
178â†’            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
179â†’            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
180â†’            emit = emit,
181â†’        )
182â†’    }
183â†’    private fun visitClass(
184â†’        node: ASTNode,
185â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
186â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
187â†’    ) {
188â†’        val psi = node.psi
189â†’        require(psi is KtClass)
190â†’
191â†’        node
192â†’            .takeIf { psi.isEnum() }
193â†’            ?.findChildByType(ElementType.CLASS_BODY)
194â†’            ?.takeUnless {
195â†’                it.noEnumEntries() || it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()
196â†’            }?.let { classBody ->
197â†’                classBody
198â†’                    .findNodeAfterLastEnumEntry()
199â†’                    ?.let { nodeAfterLastEnumEntry ->
200â†’                        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
201â†’                            inspectNode = nodeAfterLastEnumEntry,
202â†’                            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
203â†’                            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
204â†’                            emit = emit,
205â†’                        )
206â†’                    }
207â†’            }
208â†’    }
209â†’
210â†’    private fun ASTNode.noEnumEntries() = children().none { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
213â†’        val lastTwoEnumEntries =
214â†’            children()
215â†’                .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
216â†’                .toList()
217â†’                .takeLast(2)
218â†’
219â†’        return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
220â†’    }
221â†’
222â†’    /**
223â†’     * Determines the [ASTNode] before which the trailing comma is allowed.
224â†’     *
225â†’     * If the list of enumeration entries is terminated by a semicolon, that semicolon will be returned. Otherwise, the
226â†’     * last element of the class.
227â†’     */
228â†’    private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterLastEnumEntry() =
229â†’        children()
230â†’            .lastOrNull { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
231â†’            ?.children()
232â†’            ?.singleOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.SEMICOLON }
233â†’            ?: lastChildNode
234â†’
235â†’    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
236â†’        inspectNode: ASTNode,
237â†’        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
238â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
239â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
240â†’    ) {
241â†’        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
242â†’        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
243â†’        val trailingCommaState = when {
244â†’            isMultiline(psi) -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
245â†’            else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
246â†’        }
247â†’        when (trailingCommaState) {
248â†’            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
249â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
250â†’                    inspectNode
251â†’                        .treeParent
252â†’                        .takeIf { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
253â†’                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
254â†’                        ?.prevLeaf()
255â†’                        ?.let { lastNodeBeforeArrow ->
256â†’                            if (lastNodeBeforeArrow.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !lastNodeBeforeArrow.textContains('\n')) {
257â†’                                emit(
258â†’                                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
259â†’                                    "Expected a newline between the trailing comma and  \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
260â†’                                    true,
261â†’                                )
262â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
263â†’                                    val parentIndent = "\n" + inspectNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
264â†’                                    lastNodeBeforeArrow.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(parentIndent)
265â†’                                }
266â†’                            }
267â†’                        }
268â†’                } else {
269â†’                    emit(
270â†’                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
271â†’                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
272â†’                        true,
273â†’                    )
274â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
275â†’                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
276â†’                    }
277â†’                }
278â†’            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
279â†’                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
280â†’                    val addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry = addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen()
281â†’                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282â†’                    if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
283â†’                        emit(
284â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
285â†’                            "Missing trailing comma and newline before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
286â†’                            true,
287â†’                        )
288â†’                    } else {
289â†’                        emit(
290â†’                            prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
291â†’                            "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
292â†’                            true,
293â†’                        )
294â†’                    }
295â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
296â†’                        if (addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhenEntry) {
297â†’                            val parentIndent = "\n" + prevNode.getWhenEntryIndent()
298â†’                            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
299â†’                            if (leafBeforeArrow != null && leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace) {
300â†’                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
301â†’                                leafBeforeArrow.replace(newLine)
302â†’                            } else {
303â†’                                val newLine = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
304â†’                                prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(newLine, prevNode.psi)
305â†’                            }
306â†’                        }
307â†’
308â†’                        val comma = KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi).createComma()
309â†’                        if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
310â†’                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
311â†’                                val parentIndent =
312â†’                                    (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
313â†’                                        ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
314â†’                                val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
315â†’                                val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
316â†’                                enumEntry.apply {
317â†’                                    inspectNode.psi.replace(comma)
318â†’                                    add(newline)
319â†’                                    add(createSemicolon())
320â†’                                }
321â†’                            }
322â†’                            Unit
323â†’                        } else {
324â†’                            prevNode.psi.parent.addAfter(comma, prevNode.psi)
325â†’                        }
326â†’                    }
327â†’                }
328â†’            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
329â†’                emit(
330â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
331â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
332â†’                    true,
333â†’                )
334â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
335â†’                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
336â†’                }
337â†’            }
338â†’            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
339â†’        }
340â†’    }
341â†’
342â†’    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
343â†’        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
344â†’        parents()
345â†’            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }
346â†’            .lineIndent()
347â†’
348â†’    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
349â†’        when {
350â†’            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
351â†’                isMultiline(element.parent)
352â†’            }
353â†’            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
354â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
355â†’            }
356â†’            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
357â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
358â†’            }
359â†’            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
360â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
361â†’            }
362â†’            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
363â†’                element.children.size == 1 &&
364â†’                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
365â†’                // special handling for collection literal
366â†’                // @Annotation([
367â†’                //    "something",
368â†’                // ])
369â†’                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
370â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
371â†’            }
372â†’            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
373â†’                false
374â†’            }
375â†’            else -> {
376â†’                element.textContains('\n')
377â†’            }
378â†’        }
379â†’
380â†’    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
381â†’        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
382â†’            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
383â†’            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
384â†’        } else {
385â†’            false
386â†’        }
387â†’
388â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
389â†’        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
390â†’            this
391â†’        } else {
392â†’            prevCodeLeaf()
393â†’        }
394â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
395â†’    }
396â†’
397â†’    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
398â†’        from: PsiElement,
399â†’        to: PsiElement,
400â†’    ): Boolean {
401â†’        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
402â†’        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
403â†’            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
404â†’                return true
405â†’            }
406â†’            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
407â†’        }
408â†’        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
409â†’    }
410â†’
411â†’    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
412â†’        /**
413â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
414â†’         */
415â†’        EXISTS,
416â†’
417â†’        /**
418â†’         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
419â†’         */
420â†’        MISSING,
421â†’
422â†’        /**
423â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
424â†’         */
425â†’        NOT_EXISTS,
426â†’
427â†’        /**
428â†’         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
429â†’         */
430â†’        REDUNDANT,
431â†’    }
432â†’

434â†’        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")

437â†’            EditorConfigProperty(

439â†’                    "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma",

442â†’                        "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +

 36â†’/**
 37â†’ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38â†’ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41â†’ *
 42â†’ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43â†’ */
 44â†’public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46â†’        node: ASTNode,
 47â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49â†’    ) {
 50â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 51â†’            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52â†’                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53â†’            }
 54â†’            ANNOTATION -> {
 55â†’                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56â†’            }
 57â†’            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58â†’                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59â†’        }
 60â†’    }
 61â†’
 62â†’    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63â†’        node: ASTNode,
 64â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66â†’    ) {
 67â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68â†’
 69â†’        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72â†’            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73â†’            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74â†’        ) {
 75â†’            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76â†’        }
 77â†’
 78â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80â†’        ) {
 81â†’            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82â†’        }
 83â†’
 84â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85â†’            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86â†’            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87â†’        ) {
 88â†’            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89â†’        }
 90â†’    }
 91â†’
 92â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93â†’        node: ASTNode,
 94â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96â†’    ) {
 97â†’        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98â†’            emit(
 99â†’                node.startOffset,
100â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101â†’                true,
102â†’            )
103â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
104â†’                node
105â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106â†’                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107â†’            }
108â†’        }
109â†’
110â†’        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111â†’            emit(
112â†’                node.startOffset,
113â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114â†’                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115â†’                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116â†’                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117â†’                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119â†’            )
120â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
121â†’                node
122â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123â†’                    .nextLeaf()
124â†’                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125â†’                    ?.let {
126â†’                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127â†’                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128â†’                        } else {
129â†’                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130â†’                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131â†’                            )
132â†’                        }
133â†’                    }
134â†’            }
135â†’        }
136â†’    }
137â†’
138â†’    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139â†’        node: ASTNode,
140â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142â†’    ) {
143â†’        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144â†’            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145â†’                node
146â†’                    .siblings(forward = false)
147â†’                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148â†’            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149â†’                emit(
150â†’                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151â†’                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152â†’                    true,
153â†’                )
154â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
155â†’                    node
156â†’                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157â†’                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158â†’                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159â†’                }
160â†’            }
161â†’        }
162â†’    }
163â†’
164â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ) {
169â†’        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170â†’            node
171â†’                .siblings(forward = true)
172â†’                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173â†’        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174â†’            node
175â†’                .siblings(forward = false)
176â†’                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177â†’        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178â†’            emit(
179â†’                node.startOffset,
180â†’                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181â†’                true,
182â†’            )
183â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
184â†’                node
185â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186â†’                    .nextLeaf()
187â†’                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188â†’            }
189â†’        }
190â†’    }
191â†’
192â†’    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193â†’
194â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195â†’        psi
196â†’            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197â†’            ?.useSiteTarget
198â†’            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()

288â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first

293â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296â†’        } else {

136â†’    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
137â†’        inspectNode: ASTNode,
138â†’        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
139â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,

145â†’            this.isMultiline() -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING

149â†’            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS -> if (!isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
150â†’                emit(

152â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
153â†’                    true,

161â†’                emit(
162â†’                    prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,

174â†’                emit(
175â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
176â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
177â†’                    true,
178â†’                )
179â†’                if (autoCorrect) {

 28â†’private const val KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST = "KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST"
 29â†’private const val KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE = "KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE" // Keep value in sync with value in 'logback-test.xml'
 30â†’private const val KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_ON_PROPERTY = "ON"
 31â†’
 32â†’private val LOGGER =
 33â†’    KotlinLogging
 34â†’        .logger {}
 35â†’        .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
 36â†’            if (!logger.isTraceEnabled || !logger.isDebugEnabled) {
 37â†’                logger.info {
 38â†’                    """
 39â†’                    Additional information can be printed during running of unit tests, by setting one or more of environment variables below:
 40â†’                        $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE=[on|off]
 41â†’                        $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST=[on|off]
 42â†’                    """.trimIndent()
 43â†’                }
 44â†’            }
 45â†’        }.initKtLintKLogger()
 46â†’
 47â†’private val DUMP_AST_RULE_PROVIDER =
 48â†’    System
 49â†’        .getenv(KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST)
 50â†’        .orEmpty()
 51â†’        .equals(KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_ON_PROPERTY, ignoreCase = true)
 52â†’        .ifTrue {
 53â†’            LOGGER.info { "Dump AST of code before processing as System environment variable $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_DUMP_AST is set to 'on'" }
 54â†’            RuleProvider {
 55â†’                DumpASTRule(
 56â†’                    // Write to STDOUT. The focus in a failed unit test should first go to the error in the rule that is
 57â†’                    // to be tested and not to the AST,
 58â†’                    out = System.out,
 59â†’                )
 60â†’            }
 61â†’        }.let { setOfNotNull(it) }
 62â†’
 63â†’// The execution of the unit tests may not be affected by the ".editorconfig" configuration of the Ktlint project itself. So each unit test
 64â†’// is to be run on a KtlintTestFileSystem not having any ".editorconfig" files. This variable should not be exposed, as unit tests should
 65â†’// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
 66â†’// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
 67â†’private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()
 68â†’
 69â†’/**
 70â†’ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 71â†’ *
 72â†’ * ```
 73â†’ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 74â†’ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 75â†’ *
 76â†’ * @Test
 77â†’ * fun `Some test`() {
 78â†’ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 79â†’ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 80â†’ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 81â†’ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 82â†’ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 83â†’ * }
 84â†’ * ```
 85â†’ */
 86â†’@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 87â†’public class KtLintAssertThat(
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 90â†’     */
 91â†’    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 92â†’    /**
 93â†’     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 94â†’     */
 95â†’    private val code: String,
 96â†’    /**
 97â†’     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 98â†’     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 99â†’     */
100â†’    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
101â†’) {
102â†’    private var filePath: String? = null
103â†’    private var kotlinScript = false
104â†’    private var editorConfigProperties = emptySet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>()
105â†’
106â†’    /**
107â†’     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
108â†’     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
109â†’     */
110â†’    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
111â†’        editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties + properties.toSet()
112â†’
113â†’        return this
114â†’    }
115â†’
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
118â†’     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
119â†’     * silently overwritten.
120â†’     *
121â†’     * Example of usage:
122â†’     * ```
123â†’     *  val code =
124â†’     *      """
125â†’     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
126â†’     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
127â†’     *      """.trimIndent()
128â†’     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
129â†’     *      .setMaxLineLength()
130â†’     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
131â†’     * ```
132â†’     */
133â†’    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
134â†’    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
135â†’        code
136â†’            .split("\n")
137â†’            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
138â†’            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
139â†’            ?.let { index ->
140â†’                editorConfigProperties =
141â†’                    editorConfigProperties + setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString())
142â†’            } ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
143â†’
144â†’        return this
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    /**
148â†’     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
149â†’     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
150â†’     */
151â†’    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
152â†’        this.filePath = filePath
153â†’        return this
154â†’    }
155â†’
156â†’    /**
157â†’     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
158â†’     */
159â†’    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
160â†’        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
161â†’        return this
162â†’    }
163â†’
164â†’    /**
165â†’     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
166â†’     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
167â†’     * indenting.
168â†’     *
169â†’     * Caution:
170â†’     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
171â†’     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
172â†’     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
173â†’     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
174â†’     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
175â†’     *
176â†’     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
177â†’     */
178â†’    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
179â†’        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
180â†’
181â†’        return this
182â†’    }
183â†’
184â†’    /**
185â†’     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
186â†’     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
187â†’     * indenting.
188â†’     *
189â†’     * Caution:
190â†’     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
191â†’     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
192â†’     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
193â†’     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
194â†’     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
195â†’     *
196â†’     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
197â†’     */
198â†’    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
199â†’        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
200â†’            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
201â†’        )
202â†’
203â†’        return this
204â†’    }
205â†’
206â†’    /**
207â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
208â†’     *
209â†’     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
210â†’     */
211â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
212â†’
213â†’    /**
214â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
215â†’     */
216â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
217â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
218â†’
219â†’    /**
220â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
221â†’     *
222â†’     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
223â†’     */
224â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
225â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
226â†’
227â†’    /**
228â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
229â†’     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
230â†’     */
231â†’    public fun hasLintViolation(
232â†’        line: Int,
233â†’        col: Int,
234â†’        detail: String,
235â†’    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolation(line, col, detail)
236â†’
237â†’    /**
238â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected. Note that tests
239â†’     * resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
240â†’     */
241â†’    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
242â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolations(*expectedErrors)
243â†’
244â†’    /**
245â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule which automatically can be
246â†’     * corrected. This is a sugar coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one
247â†’     * lint violation.
248â†’     */
249â†’    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
250â†’        line: Int,
251â†’        col: Int,
252â†’        detail: String,
253â†’    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(line, col, detail)
254â†’
255â†’    /**
256â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by an additional rules which can be
257â†’     * automatically corrected. Note that tests resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to
258â†’     * comprehend.
259â†’     */
260â†’    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRule(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
261â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(*expectedErrors)
262â†’
263â†’    /**
264â†’     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
265â†’     */
266â†’    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
267â†’
268â†’    /**
269â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
270â†’     */
271â†’    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
272â†’        line: Int,
273â†’        col: Int,
274â†’        detail: String,
275â†’    ): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(line, col, detail)
276â†’
277â†’    /**
278â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s which can not be automatically corrected. Note that
279â†’     * tests resulting in only one [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
280â†’     */
281â†’    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): Unit =
282â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(*expectedErrors)
283â†’
284â†’    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
285â†’        if (editorConfigProperties.isEmpty()) {
286â†’            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
287â†’                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
288â†’                code = code(),
289â†’                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
290â†’                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
291â†’            )
292â†’        } else {
293â†’            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
294â†’                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
295â†’                code = code(),
296â†’                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(*editorConfigProperties.toTypedArray()),
297â†’                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
298â†’            )
299â†’        }
300â†’
301â†’    private fun code() =
302â†’        if (filePath != null) {
303â†’            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
304â†’            // the Ktlint test file system.
305â†’            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
306â†’            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
307â†’            Code.fromPath(path)
308â†’        } else {
309â†’            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)
310â†’        }
311â†’
312â†’    public companion object {
313â†’        /**
314â†’         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
315â†’         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
316â†’         */
317â†’        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
318â†’
319â†’        /**
320â†’         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
321â†’         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
322â†’         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
323â†’         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
324â†’         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
325â†’         */
326â†’
327â†’        public fun assertThatRule(
328â†’            provider: () -> Rule,
329â†’            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
330â†’        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders)
331â†’
332â†’        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet()): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
333â†’            { code ->
334â†’                KtLintAssertThat(
335â†’                    ruleProvider = this,
336â†’                    code = code,
337â†’                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
338â†’                )
339â†’            }
340â†’
341â†’        /**
342â†’         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
343â†’         */
344â†’        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
345â†’
346â†’        /**
347â†’         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
348â†’         */
349â†’        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
350â†’
351â†’        init {
352â†’            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
353â†’             * val code =
354â†’             *     """
355â†’             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
356â†’             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
357â†’             *     """.trimIndent()
358â†’             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
359â†’             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
360â†’             */
361â†’            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
362â†’                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
363â†’                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
364â†’            }
365â†’        }
366â†’    }
367â†’}
368â†’
369â†’/**
370â†’ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
371â†’ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
372â†’ * assertions operate on the same state.
373â†’ */
374â†’public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
375â†’    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
376â†’    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
377â†’    private val code: Code,
378â†’    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
379â†’    /**
380â†’     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
381â†’     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
382â†’     */
383â†’    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
384â†’) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
385â†’    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
386â†’
387â†’    /**
388â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
389â†’     * KtLintAssertThat.
390â†’     *
391â†’     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
392â†’     */
393â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
394â†’        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
395â†’
396â†’        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
397â†’        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
398â†’
399â†’        assertAll(
400â†’            {
401â†’                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
402â†’                    .describedAs(
403â†’                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
404â†’                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
405â†’                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
406â†’                            } else {
407â†’                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
408â†’                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
409â†’                            },
410â†’                    )
411â†’                    .isEmpty()
412â†’            },
413â†’            {
414â†’                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
415â†’                    .describedAs(
416â†’                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
417â†’                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
418â†’                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
419â†’                            } else {
420â†’                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
421â†’                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
422â†’                            },
423â†’                    )
424â†’                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
425â†’            },
426â†’        )
427â†’    }
428â†’
429â†’    /**
430â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
431â†’     */
432â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
433â†’        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
434â†’
435â†’        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
436â†’            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '$ruleId' while none were expected")
437â†’            .isEmpty()
438â†’
439â†’        return this
440â†’    }
441â†’
442â†’    /**
443â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
444â†’     *
445â†’     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
446â†’     * formatted.
447â†’     *
448â†’     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
449â†’     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
450â†’     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
451â†’     * violated.
452â†’     */
453â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
454â†’        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
455â†’            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
456â†’        }
457â†’
458â†’        val lintErrors = lint()
459â†’        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
460â†’        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
461â†’
462â†’        return this
463â†’    }
464â†’
465â†’    /**
466â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
467â†’     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
468â†’     */
469â†’    public fun hasLintViolation(
470â†’        line: Int,
471â†’        col: Int,
472â†’        detail: String,
473â†’    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
474â†’        hasLintViolations(
475â†’            LintViolation(
476â†’                line = line,
477â†’                col = col,
478â†’                detail = detail,
479â†’            ),
480â†’        )
481â†’
482â†’    /**
483â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
484â†’     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
485â†’     */
486â†’    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
487â†’        line: Int,
488â†’        col: Int,
489â†’        detail: String,
490â†’    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
491â†’        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
492â†’            LintViolation(
493â†’                line = line,
494â†’                col = col,
495â†’                detail = detail,
496â†’            ),
497â†’        )
498â†’
499â†’    /**
500â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
501â†’     */
502â†’    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
503â†’        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
504â†’
505â†’        val actualLintViolationFields =
506â†’            lint()
507â†’                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
508â†’                .toLintViolationsFields()
509â†’        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
510â†’            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
511â†’            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
512â†’        return this
513â†’    }
514â†’
515â†’    /**

586â†’    // The test below covers for a bug which was quite hard to track down as it was a combination of two distinct bugs.
587â†’    // Code sample before formatting:
588â†’    //     val foo = foo(
589â†’    //         listOf(
590â†’    //             "bar",
591â†’    //         )
592â†’    //     )
593â†’    // Code sample after formatting (not wrong indenting of closing parenthesis after element "bar"):
594â†’    //     val foo = foo(
595â†’    //         listOf(
596â†’    //             "bar",
597â†’    //             ),
598â†’    //     )
599â†’    // The problem could not be reproduced within the IndentationRuleTest. This was caused by a problem regarding the
600â†’    // sort order of the rules. The code sample above was formatted with CLI parameter "--experimental" while in the
601â†’    // IndentationRuleTest the "experimental function-signature" was not added as additional rule. Without the
602â†’    // FunctionSignatureRule the effective order of the rules became:
603â†’    //   - standard:wrapping,
604â†’    //   - standard:indent,
605â†’    //   - standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site,
606â†’    // This is wrong as the indent rule may only run after the trailing comma rules. The problem was that the Rule
607â†’    // sorter did not take rules having multiple RunAfterRule modifier into account.
608â†’    //
609â†’    // The second problem was a problem in the TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule. The problem can only be visualized by
610â†’    // inspecting the actual Psi which was generated. Before fixing this bug, the trailing comma was added as an element
611â†’    // of the PsiElement representing the 'listOf("bar")' instead of as an element of the PsiElement representing the
612â†’    // 'foo(...)'. This caused the IndentationRule to format the closing parenthesis after element "bar" incorrectly.
613â†’    @Test
614â†’    fun `Given a function call with a list parameter then add the trailing comma after the PsiElement representing the list`() {
615â†’        val code =
616â†’            """
617â†’            val foo = foo(
618â†’                listOf(
619â†’                    "bar",
620â†’                )
621â†’            )
622â†’            """.trimIndent()
623â†’        val formattedCode =
624â†’            """
625â†’            val foo = foo(
626â†’                listOf(
627â†’                    "bar",
628â†’                ),
629â†’            )
630â†’            """.trimIndent()
631â†’        trailingCommaOnCallSiteRuleAssertThat(code)
632â†’            .withEditorConfigOverride(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY to true)
633â†’            .hasLintViolation(4, 6, "Missing trailing comma before \")\"")
634â†’            .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
635â†’    }
